Performance is measured through three key indicators: Average CPI for Managed Projects (average Earned Value divided by Actual Cost across all active projects, target 0.95 or higher), Average SPI for Managed Projects (average Earned Value divided by Planned Value across all active projects, target 0.9 or higher), and Progress Report Submission Rate (reports submitted on time divided by total required reports, multiplied by 100, target 100%).

name: Execution Teams
short_description: Performs construction and installation works on site under supervision.
content: Execution Teams report to the Project Manager and Site Supervisor, and require specialized technical and professional qualifications according to work nature, practical field experience, and commitment to HSE requirements. Execution Teams perform field work according to drawings and instructions, comply with quality and safety requirements, submit daily progress reports to the supervisor or Project Manager, maintain equipment and materials on site, report any obstacles or risks during execution, follow instructions from the Project Manager and supervisors, participate in inspection and acceptance, maintain site cleanliness and organization, adhere to work hours and productivity plans, and support closure of technical observations or client comments. Performance is measured through three key indicators: Actual vs Planned Achievement Rate (executed quantities divided by planned quantities for the period, multiplied by 100, target 90% or higher), Site Incident Rate (number of safety incidents divided by total work hours, multiplied by 200,000, target below 1.0), and Execution Quality (accepted inspections from first time divided by total inspections, multiplied by 100, target 90% or higher).

name: Engineering and Design Manager
short_description: Leads engineering design, reviews drawings, and supports tenders and technical proposals.
content: The Engineering and Design Manager reports to the Project Management Manager and requires a Bachelor's degree in Civil, Electrical, or Mechanical Engineering, at least 10 years of engineering design experience with 4 years in supervision, and proficiency in AutoCAD or equivalent. The Engineering and Design Manager manages design and engineering work for projects, reviews and approves drawings and technical calculations, supports technical proposals and tenders, coordinates with project managers to resolve technical issues, manages design changes during execution, ensures design compliance with specifications and codes, reviews execution drawings, supports technical acceptance and testing, manages the engineering and design team, and submits technical reports to the Project Management Manager. Performance is measured through three key indicators: Drawings Delivered On-Time Rate (drawings delivered on time divided by total required drawings, multiplied by 100, target 90% or higher), Design Review Acceptance Rate (designs accepted from first review divided by total designs, multiplied by 100, target 80% or higher), and Change Orders Due to Design Errors (number of scope changes issued due to design errors, target 2 per project).

name: Supporting Common Functions
short_description: Includes site engineers, office engineers, supervisors, document controllers, HSE officers, drivers, technicians, and workers.
content: This section covers supporting common functions that assist project execution and site operations, including site engineers, office engineers, supervisors, document controllers, HSE officers, drivers, technicians, and workers.

name: Site Engineer
short_description: Supervises technical aspects of field work, ensures compliance with drawings and specifications.
content: The Site Engineer reports to the Project Manager or Site Supervisor and requires a Bachelor's degree in Civil, Electrical, or Mechanical Engineering and at least 3 years of field engineering experience. The Site Engineer supervises daily site technical activities, ensures work is executed according to approved drawings and specifications, coordinates with the consultant and subcontractors for technical matters, prepares site inspection reports and daily logs, manages quality control and testing activities on site, resolves site technical issues and coordinates with the office engineer, monitors material delivery and compliance with technical specifications, participates in site meetings and progress reviews, ensures safety and quality standards are followed, and prepares progress reports and assists in claim preparation. Performance is measured through three key indicators: Work Compliance with Specifications (inspections passed divided by total inspections, multiplied by 100, target 95% or higher), Daily Reporting Compliance (reports submitted on time divided by total work days, multiplied by 100, target 95% or higher), and Issue Resolution Time (average days to resolve site technical issues, target 2 days or less).

name: Office Engineer for Project
short_description: Prepares and reviews engineering documents, manages drawing registers, and supports technical coordination.
content: The Office Engineer for Project reports to the Project Manager or Engineering Manager and requires a Bachelor's degree in Civil, Electrical, or Mechanical Engineering, at least 3 years of technical office experience, and proficiency in AutoCAD and MS Project. The Office Engineer reviews engineering drawings and ensures compliance with specifications and contract, prepares and distributes transmittal and receipt documents, follows up on drawing requests and shop details from site, prepares and updates Drawing Register and approved versions, prepares engineering memos and Requests for Information, reviews Material Submittals and submits to consultant, supports progress invoice preparation and quantity takeoffs, coordinates with the Site Engineer to resolve emerging technical issues, prepares weekly and monthly engineering status reports, maintains electronic archive of drawings and technical documents, and supports preparation of As-Built documents and Operation & Maintenance manuals. Performance is measured through four key indicators: Drawings Reviewed On-Time Rate (drawings reviewed on time divided by total received drawings, multiplied by 100, target 95% or higher), Documents Returned Due to Technical Errors Rate (documents returned due to errors divided by total sent documents, multiplied by 100, target 5% or less), RFI Response Time (average working days from request receipt to response, target 3 working days), and Weekly Progress Reporting Compliance (reports submitted on time divided by total required reports, multiplied by 100, target 100%).

name: Site Supervisor
short_description: Oversees daily site work, directs teams, and ensures compliance with quality and safety.
content: The Site Supervisor reports to the Project Manager and requires a Diploma in Civil Engineering or related field, at least 5 years of site supervision experience, and knowledge of construction methods and safety procedures. The Site Supervisor provides daily supervision of site work, distributes tasks to labor and technicians, ensures work is executed according to drawings, monitors daily productivity, reports material or equipment needs, ensures safety compliance, coordinates inspections with QA/QC, prepares daily reports of executed work, handles minor field issues, escalates obstacles to the Project Manager, verifies material suitability before use, supervises application of work permits and safety procedures, documents executed quantities daily, and participates in weekly site meetings. Performance is measured through four key indicators: Daily Achievement vs Plan Rate (daily executed quantities divided by planned quantities, multiplied by 100, target 90% or higher), Site Safety Incidents (number of documented incidents on site during the period, target zero major incidents), First-Time Inspection Acceptance Rate (inspections accepted without observations divided by total inspections, multiplied by 100, target 85% or higher), and Progress Report Submission Rate (reports submitted on time divided by total required reports, multiplied by 100, target 100%).

name: Administrative Coordinator / Document Controller
short_description: Organizes documents, manages correspondence, and ensures version control and archiving.
content: The Administrative Coordinator / Document Controller reports to the Project Manager or Sector Manager and is responsible for organizing and maintaining physical and electronic documents. The Administrative Coordinator issues and tracks official correspondence, manages document copies and ensures use of latest versions, follows up on approvals and signatures, prepares incoming and outgoing registers, supports report and meeting preparation, archives contracts and drawings, ensures document confidentiality and protection, coordinates between departments when needed, reports document deficiencies or delays, applies uniform document numbering and classification systems, ensures drawings and technical documents are distributed to relevant parties immediately upon receipt, monitors and updates Document Distribution Matrix, and prepares periodic reports on pending or unapproved documents. Performance is measured through four key indicators: Properly Archived Documents Rate (documents archived per system divided by total received documents, multiplied by 100, target 99% or higher), Document Retrieval Time (average minutes from document request to delivery, target 15 minutes or less), Correspondence Processed On-Time Rate (correspondence processed same day divided by total correspondence, multiplied by 100, target 95% or higher), and Document Register Accuracy (documents with correct version divided by total covered documents, multiplied by 100, target 100%).

name: Occupational Health and Safety Officer (HSE Officer) - Field
short_description: Implements and monitors HSE systems at project sites, conducts inspections, and ensures compliance.
section_path: 15.5 Occupational Health and Safety Officer (HSE Officer) - Field
content: The Occupational Health and Safety Officer (Field) reports to the Project Manager and QHSE Manager and requires a Bachelor's degree in Engineering or Occupational Health and Safety, NEBOSH or IOSH certification, and at least 3 years of HSE experience in construction or industrial projects. The HSE Officer develops and implements project-specific occupational health and safety plans, conducts daily site inspection tours to identify safety hazards, organizes and delivers safety awareness and training programs for all site workers, investigates incidents and injuries and prepares root cause analysis, supervises provision and use of Personal Protective Equipment, coordinates with government and regulatory bodies, prepares and updates safety records and performance reports, reviews work plans for safety compliance, supervises storage and handling of hazardous materials, leads emergency drills and evacuation plans, and follows up on work permits for high-risk activities. Performance is measured through three key indicators: Site Incident Rate (number of incidents per month, target 0 incidents per month), Safety Standards Compliance Rate (inspection findings, target 95% or higher), and Safety Training Completion Rate (trained workers divided by total workers, multiplied by 100, target 100%).

name: Equipment/Vehicle Driver
short_description: Operates and transports heavy equipment or vehicles, ensuring safety and maintenance.
content: The Equipment/Vehicle Driver reports to the Site Supervisor or Equipment and Maintenance Manager and requires a valid driving license appropriate for the equipment or vehicle, at least 2 years of experience operating similar equipment, and familiarity with site safety procedures. The Equipment/Vehicle Driver operates assigned heavy equipment or vehicles efficiently and safely, conducts daily pre-operation inspection and reports any malfunctions, transports materials and equipment between sites according to operational schedule, complies with traffic safety rules and site instructions, maintains vehicle or equipment cleanliness and performs basic periodic maintenance, delivers and receives equipment to maintenance department when needed, signs daily operation logs, and immediately reports any incident or malfunction. Performance is measured through four key indicators: Actual Operating Hours Rate (actual operating hours divided by planned hours, multiplied by 100, target 90% or higher), Breakdown Rate During Operation (number of breakdowns recorded during the month, target 1 breakdown per month), Daily Operation Log Compliance (completed logs divided by total operation days, multiplied by 100, target 100%), and Operational Safety (number of operation-related incidents, target zero incidents).

name: Specialist Technician
short_description: Performs specialized technical work (electrical, mechanical, welding, etc.) under supervision.
content: The Specialist Technician reports to the Site Supervisor or Site Engineer and requires a Diploma or professional certification in the relevant specialization, at least 2 years of field experience, and the ability to read basic technical drawings. The Specialist Technician performs specialized technical work according to approved drawings and specifications, conducts initial inspections and tests of equipment and systems, reports any technical issues to the Site Supervisor immediately upon discovery, maintains and cares for tools and equipment, complies with quality standards and safety procedures, participates in inspection and handover activities with QA/QC team, documents completed work daily, and trains new workers on basic tasks when needed. Performance is measured through four key indicators: First-Time Work Quality Acceptance Rate (accepted work divided by total executed work, multiplied by 100, target 90% or higher), Rework Rate (rework hours divided by total work hours, multiplied by 100, target 5% or less), Daily Work Report Compliance (reports submitted divided by work days, multiplied by 100, target 100%), and Work Safety (number of technician-related incidents, target zero incidents).

name: Worker
short_description: Performs manual and operational tasks at project sites, supporting execution teams.
content: The Worker reports to the Site Supervisor and requires no specific educational qualification, but must be able to perform manual work and commit to safety instructions. The Worker performs excavation, backfilling, and manual handling work according to supervisor instructions, assists technicians in preparing work sites and arranging materials, maintains site cleanliness and organization, moves materials and tools between different site areas, wears Personal Protective Equipment at all times, immediately reports any hazards or incidents to the direct supervisor, participates in safety awareness and training programs, and performs any other tasks assigned by the direct supervisor. Performance is measured through four key indicators: Daily Productivity (actually executed quantities divided by planned quantities, multiplied by 100, target 85% or higher), Attendance and Discipline Compliance (actual attendance days divided by scheduled work days, multiplied by 100, target 95% or higher), Safety Standards Compliance (number of safety violations recorded against the worker, target zero violations), and Work Quality (number of positive observations from supervisor on work quality, target 2 observations per month).

name: Abbreviated Administrative Escalation Matrix
short_description: Provides a quick reference for reporting lines and escalation paths for each function.
content: The Abbreviated Administrative Escalation Matrix provides a quick reference for reporting lines and escalation paths for each function within the organization. The CEO reports to the Board of Directors. The QHSE Manager, PMO Manager, Legal and Contracts Affairs Manager, Business Development Manager, Finance Director, Human Resources Manager, Operations Manager, and Project Management Manager all report directly to the CEO. QA/QC Officers and HSE Officers report to the QHSE Manager. Planning, Cost Control, Reporting, Risk, and Claims Officers report to the PMO Manager. Legal Administration and Contract Management report to the Legal and Contracts Affairs Manager. Pricing, Tenders, Market Analysis, Relationship Management, and Government Tenders roles report to the Business Development Manager. Accounts Managers, General Accounts, Project Accounts, Treasury, and Credit report to the Finance Director. Payroll, Recruitment, Training, Employee Affairs, Employee Relations, Saudization, and Benefits report to the Human Resources Manager. Procurement, Warehouses, Logistics, Equipment and Maintenance, and IT functions report to the Operations Manager. Sector Managers, Project Managers, Execution Teams, Engineering and Design, Site Engineers, Office Engineers, Site Supervisors, and Administrative Coordinators report through the Project Management structure. HSE Officers in the field, Equipment Drivers, Technicians, and Workers have specific reporting lines to their respective supervisors.

name: Organizational Recommendations
short_description: Recommends separation between PMO and project management, and coordination mechanisms between departments.
content: This section provides organizational recommendations for the company. First, there should be a clear separation between the PMO and the executive project management function. The PMO role is regulatory and supervisory, encompassing planning, reporting, risk management, cost control, claims, and standardizing project management methodologies. The Project Management role is executive and operational, including sector management, project managers management, execution teams management, work execution and project delivery, and daily client relationship management during execution. Second, the following periodic coordination meetings should be established: PMO with Project Management, PMO with Finance, PMO with Legal and Contracts, Business Development with Engineering, Finance, and Operations, and QHSE with Projects and Operations. A weekly coordination meeting between PMO and Project Managers should be held to review work progress. The company should implement an ERP system or unified project management program to improve information flow, and establish an internal communication platform to enhance inter-departmental coordination.

name: Guide Approval
short_description: Details the approval process and review schedule for the guide.
content: This guide becomes effective upon approval by the CEO and may be updated upon any change in the organizational structure or scope of departmental work. The guide shall be reviewed annually or upon any substantial structural change in the company. An approved copy of the guide shall be sent to all department managers upon approval. This guide is adopted as the official reference for recruitment, evaluation, and training decisions. The guide has been prepared by Eng. Mohammed Saleh Hassoun, reviewed by Human Resources Management, and approved by the Board of Directors or CEO, as indicated on the Approval Page.
